method_atom: a framework for quantifying the asymptotic exponential decay rates (error exponents) of the two possible misclassification probabilities in a binary decision problem that chooses between two candidate probabilistic descriptions from a growing sequence of observations, generalizing bounds on the optimal error trade-off and overlap (of Chernoff and Hoeffding type) from the independent-and-identically-distributed case to sequences whose joint description factorizes approximately over blocks (an asymptotic-independence / decay-of-correlations condition).
object_atom: the quantum states of a one-dimensional chain of coupled two-level systems (spins), specifically globally correlated states such as thermal Gibbs states of translation-invariant finite-range interactions and certain finitely correlated states.
brief_reason: The hypothesis-testing error-exponent machinery under a factorization/decay-of-correlations condition is transferable across fields; the correlated quantum spin-chain states (Gibbs and finitely correlated) are the physics-specific object, with only mild fusion since the bounds are stated in quantum (non-commutative) form.
